other_findings.finding: Dermal Tregs in adult skin show high mRNA and protein expression of CD62L (SELL), the circulating central memory T cells marker, distinguishing them from epidermal T cells
In healthy adult human skin, approximately 20% of tissue-resident CD4+ T-cells are Tregs expressing Foxp3, and only 5% of Tregs recirculate
Tregs suppress overactive immunity, enable tissue tolerance, and restore homeostasis. They increase EGFR expression favouring wound re-epithelialisation

evidence_quotes.source_file: Reynolds et al. (2021) PDF full text

evidence_quotes.quote: Dermal Tregs show high mRNA and protein expression of the circulating central memory T cells marker CD62L (SELL)

evidence_quotes.quote: Tregs expressed FOXP3, CTLA4 and IL2RA, defining their immune-modulatory function
evidence_quotes.context: Treg markers in healthy human skin
